message:
fix: Wrap labeled blocks in parentheses for function argument safety

When labeled blocks (e.g., dkeys_N:, dvals_N:, ditems_N:, attr_N:) are
used as function arguments, the label syntax can be confused with named
arguments in Zig. Fix by wrapping all such expressions in parentheses.

Fixes:
- dict.keys(), dict.values(), dict.items() - wrap block in parentheses
- attribute access on block expressions - wrap in parentheses
- assertRaisesRegex attribute callable handling
- assertRaisesRegex lambda callable handling

This prevents the "expected ',' after argument" error when these
expressions are used as function parameters.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+# Run CPython tests and categorize results
+
+passed=0
+failed=0
+errors=""
+
+for f in tests/cpython/test_*.py; do
+    name=$(basename "$f" .py)
+    result=$(./zig-out/bin/metal0 "$f" --force 2>&1)
+    if echo "$result" | grep -q "Compiled successfully"; then
+        echo "OK: $name"
+        passed=$((passed + 1))
+    else
+        echo "FAIL: $name"
+        failed=$((failed + 1))
+        # Get first error
+        err=$(echo "$result" | grep "error:" | head -1)
+        errors="$errors\n$name: $err"
+    fi
+done
+
+echo ""
+echo "================================"
+echo "Passed: $passed"
+echo "Failed: $failed"
+echo "================================"
